title: Intro to knntuner
text: KNN model workflows often involve repeatedly creating cross-validation controls, tuning grids, extracting best-performing k value tedious repetitions. knntuner package provides several small helper functions help streamline process.

title: Intro to knntuner
text: can install development version knntuner GitHub : Common packages knntuner works loaded .
code: # install.packages("remotes") # install.packages("devtools") devtools::install_github("ADC-405-S26/knntuner") library(knntuner) library(rsample) library(caret) #> Loading required package: ggplot2 #> Loading required package: lattice #>  #> Attaching package: 'caret' #> The following object is masked from 'package:rsample': #>  #>     calibration library(recipes) #> Loading required package: dplyr #>  #> Attaching package: 'dplyr' #> The following objects are masked from 'package:stats': #>  #>     filter, lag #> The following objects are masked from 'package:base': #>  #>     intersect, setdiff, setequal, union #>  #> Attaching package: 'recipes' #> The following object is masked from 'package:stats': #>  #>     step

title: Intro to knntuner
text: package, small simulated dataset 100 observations 6 variables. data measure student characteristics, tracks performance. variables included can seen . id - ID student age - age, years, student study_hours - average amount hours per week student spends studying outside class score - student’s test score, 100 private_tutoring - Indicates whether student privately tutored, outside school pass - Indicates whether student meets threshold pass, score 70.
code: summary(student_knn_data) #>        id              age         study_hours        score        #>  Min.   :  1.00   Min.   :18.00   Min.   : 0.00   Min.   : 33.86   #>  1st Qu.: 25.75   1st Qu.:19.00   1st Qu.: 4.00   1st Qu.: 58.37   #>  Median : 50.50   Median :20.00   Median : 7.00   Median : 70.50   #>  Mean   : 50.50   Mean   :20.02   Mean   : 6.85   Mean   : 70.72   #>  3rd Qu.: 75.25   3rd Qu.:21.00   3rd Qu.:10.00   3rd Qu.: 84.79   #>  Max.   :100.00   Max.   :22.00   Max.   :15.00   Max.   :100.00   #>  private_tutoring  pass    #>  NO :46           YES:53   #>  YES:54           NO :47   #>                            #>                            #>                            #>

title: Intro to knntuner
text: creating evaluating model, important begin splitting entire dataset 2 distinct sections. One section called training data, model trained. section called test data, trained model’s performance can evaluated. two kept separate, prevent models trained training data inflated performances test data. one way split data.
code: set.seed(208)  knntuner_data_split <- initial_split(data = student_knn_data, prop = 0.8)  student_train <- training(knntuner_data_split) student_test  <- testing(knntuner_data_split)

title: Intro to knntuner
text: Often building models, important make small computations, clean scale predictor variables better model performance. process called feature engineering. example, KNN method, distance-based method, important scale numeric predictors. accomplished recipe code chunk , well removing id variable analysis, relevant analysis.
code: student_recipe <- recipe(pass ~ ., data = student_train) |>   step_rm(id) |>   step_dummy(all_nominal_predictors()) |>   step_center(all_numeric_predictors()) |>   step_scale(all_numeric_predictors())

title: Intro to knntuner
text: first function knntuner comes play , called cv_control. allows users design store cross-validation specifications quickly efficiently. Also, cv_control comes default inputs commonly used STATS 205. listed . method - Identifies type cross-validation. "repeatedcv" default number - Specifies number folds make training data cross-validation. 5 default repeats - Specifies amount times iterate cross-validation process. 1 default classProbs - Indicates whether class probabilities computed. TRUE default. summaryFunction - Specifies set metrics calculate cross-validation. caret::twoClassSummary default. Thus instances, users need code cv_control() create objects.

title: Intro to knntuner
text: second function knntuner allows users quickly create k grid store cross-validation metrics value k tested. Users can specify k values want test using arguments . min_k - Specifies minimum k value test max_k - Specifies maximum k value test step - Specifies amount increase k cross-validation process example creates k grid k values 1, 3, 5, 7, 9.
code: k_grid <- kgrid(1, 9, 2) # 1 is the min_k value, 9 is the max_k value, and the step size is 2 k_grid #>   k #> 1 1 #> 2 3 #> 3 5 #> 4 7 #> 5 9

title: Intro to knntuner
text: Now, can actually train model apply cross-validation preparation. , can use caret::train() recipe, cv k_grid objects created. act inputs function, allow efficient application caret. Note case, metric used score model sensitivity.
code: set.seed(208)  student_model <- train(   student_recipe,   data = student_train,   method = "knn",   trControl = cv, # knntuner object here!   tuneGrid = k_grid, # knntuner object here!   metric = "Sens" )

title: Intro to knntuner
text: Now applied cross-validation, can look value k, search k performs best according evaluate (sensitivity ). bestk function allows us quickly easily find identify best performing k value, well corresponding metrics. 1 input: model - Specifies model results find best k example, k value 7 performed best!
code: bestk(student_model) #>   k       ROC      Sens      Spec     ROCSD     SensSD    SpecSD #> 4 7 0.9443204 0.9277778 0.8642857 0.0415595 0.06617294 0.1014877

title: Intro to knntuner
text: Now found optimal k value, next steps process train model training data, using k value 7. can use model make predictions test data, evaluate accurate model overall.

text: Evan Toth. Author, maintainer.

code: @Manual{,   title = {knntuner: Simplify Regression Model Tuning},   author = {Evan Toth},   year = {2026},   note = {R package version 0.0.0.9000},   url = {https://adc-405-s26.github.io/knntuner/}, }

title: Simplify Regression Model Tuning
text: goal knntuner allow efficient cross-validation tuning k parameters within KNN problems.

title: Simplify Regression Model Tuning
text: can install development version knntuner GitHub :

title: Simplify Regression Model Tuning
text: basic example shows knntuner can used STATS 205 KNN model building tuning workflow. First, start standard split data training set test set. example uses student dataset built package. Next, create recipe remove ID column, dummy-code categorical predictors, scale numeric predictors KNN, prepare data analysis. , define cross-validation specifications cv_control function. Next, can train cross-validate model using specifications set. Firstly, use kgrid function quickly create dataframe hold cross-validation results k value. kgrid allows choose minimum maximum k values test, well step size take. Finally, use bestk function identify best performing k value choose final model! Now, can move training final model, analyzing performance test data.
code: library(knntuner) library(rsample) library(caret) #> Loading required package: ggplot2 #> Loading required package: lattice #>  #> Attaching package: 'caret' #> The following object is masked from 'package:rsample': #>  #>     calibration library(recipes) #> Loading required package: dplyr #>  #> Attaching package: 'dplyr' #> The following objects are masked from 'package:stats': #>  #>     filter, lag #> The following objects are masked from 'package:base': #>  #>     intersect, setdiff, setequal, union #>  #> Attaching package: 'recipes' #> The following object is masked from 'package:stats': #>  #>     step # necessary packages set.seed(208)  knntuner_data_split <- initial_split(data = student_knn_data, prop = 0.8)  student_train <- training(knntuner_data_split) student_test  <- testing(knntuner_data_split) student_recipe <- recipe(pass ~ ., data = student_train) |>   step_rm(id) |>   step_dummy(all_nominal_predictors()) |>   step_center(all_numeric_predictors()) |>   step_scale(all_numeric_predictors()) cv <- cv_control() # the default arguments are a "repeatedcv" method, 5 folds, 1 repeat, classProbs = TRUE, and summaryFunction = caret::twoClassSummary set.seed(208)  k_grid <- kgrid(1, 9, 2) # knntuner function here!  student_model <- train(   student_recipe,   data = student_train,   method = "knn",   trControl = cv,   tuneGrid = k_grid,   metric = "Sens" ) bestk(student_model) #>   k       ROC      Sens      Spec     ROCSD     SensSD    SpecSD #> 4 7 0.9443204 0.9277778 0.8642857 0.0415595 0.06617294 0.1014877

what: Extract the Best Performing K Value — bestk

title: Extract the Best Performing K Value — bestk
Extract the Best Performing K Value — bestk
text: model fitted KNN model object created caret::train()

title: Extract the Best Performing K Value — bestk
text: 1-row data frame containing best-performing k value cross validation results

title: Extract the Best Performing K Value — bestk
code: example_model <- list(   results = data.frame(k = c(1, 3, 5), Sens = c(0.70, 0.76, 0.72)),   bestTune = data.frame(k = 3) ) bestk(example_model) #>   k Sens #> 2 3 0.76

title: Create Cross-Validation Settings — cv_control
text: Create Cross-Validation Settings

title: Create Cross-Validation Settings — cv_control
code: cv_control(   method = "repeatedcv",   number = 5,   repeats = 1,   classProbs = TRUE,   summaryFunction = caret::twoClassSummary )

title: Create Cross-Validation Settings — cv_control
text: method Type cross validation number Amount folds split training data repeats Amount times repeating cross validation process classProbs Logical value indicating whether class probabilities computed summaryFunction function used calculate model performance metrics

title: Create Cross-Validation Settings — cv_control
text: trainControl object can passed caret::train().

title: Create Cross-Validation Settings — cv_control
code: cv_control() #> $method #> [1] "repeatedcv" #>  #> $number #> [1] 5 #>  #> $repeats #> [1] 1 #>  #> $search #> [1] "grid" #>  #> $p #> [1] 0.75 #>  #> $initialWindow #> NULL #>  #> $horizon #> [1] 1 #>  #> $fixedWindow #> [1] TRUE #>  #> $skip #> [1] 0 #>  #> $verboseIter #> [1] FALSE #>  #> $returnData #> [1] TRUE #>  #> $returnResamp #> [1] "final" #>  #> $savePredictions #> [1] FALSE #>  #> $classProbs #> [1] TRUE #>  #> $summaryFunction #> function (data, lev = NULL, model = NULL)  #> { #>     if (length(lev) > 2) { #>         stop(paste("Your outcome has", length(lev), "levels. The twoClassSummary() function isn't appropriate.")) #>     } #>     requireNamespaceQuietStop("pROC") #>     if (!all(levels(data[, "pred"]) == lev)) { #>         stop("levels of observed and predicted data do not match") #>     } #>     rocObject <- try(pROC::roc(data$obs, data[, lev[1]], direction = ">",  #>         quiet = TRUE), silent = TRUE) #>     rocAUC <- if (inherits(rocObject, "try-error"))  #>         NA #>     else rocObject$auc #>     out <- c(rocAUC, sensitivity(data[, "pred"], data[, "obs"],  #>         lev[1]), specificity(data[, "pred"], data[, "obs"], lev[2])) #>     names(out) <- c("ROC", "Sens", "Spec") #>     out #> } #> <bytecode: 0x55f5a2dc3c60> #> <environment: namespace:caret> #>  #> $selectionFunction #> [1] "best" #>  #> $preProcOptions #> $preProcOptions$thresh #> [1] 0.95 #>  #> $preProcOptions$ICAcomp #> [1] 3 #>  #> $preProcOptions$k #> [1] 5 #>  #> $preProcOptions$freqCut #> [1] 19 #>  #> $preProcOptions$uniqueCut #> [1] 10 #>  #> $preProcOptions$cutoff #> [1] 0.9 #>  #>  #> $sampling #> NULL #>  #> $index #> NULL #>  #> $indexOut #> NULL #>  #> $indexFinal #> NULL #>  #> $timingSamps #> [1] 0 #>  #> $predictionBounds #> [1] FALSE FALSE #>  #> $seeds #> [1] NA #>  #> $adaptive #> $adaptive$min #> [1] 5 #>  #> $adaptive$alpha #> [1] 0.05 #>  #> $adaptive$method #> [1] "gls" #>  #> $adaptive$complete #> [1] TRUE #>  #>  #> $trim #> [1] FALSE #>  #> $allowParallel #> [1] TRUE #>
